Metadata.description: Spectr is an AI lab pioneering general world models — causal, multimodal systems that learn to understand and simulate the world.

models.name: Spectr RTS
models.description: Our most powerful general purpose world model yet, materially advancing the state-of-the-art in physical accuracy of world models.

models.name: Spectr Object Intelligence
models.description: Real-time object detection for complex physical environments — identifying vehicles, equipment, and activity across industrial and field operations.

About.title: Building world models to go beyond language
About.intro: Spectr is an AI lab founded in Norway, pioneering general world models — systems that learn to understand, predict, and simulate the physical world.
paragraphs: Language models learned to reason about text. World models learn to reason about reality — how scenes evolve, how objects interact, and how the world responds to action.
We believe general world models are the next great foundation model: causal, multimodal systems capable of understanding and simulating the world for humans, AIs, and robots.
Spectr RTS is our step toward that future — a general world model trained to advance the state-of-the-art in physical accuracy, developed in Norway under national control.

SpectrRts.title: Spectr RTS
SpectrRts.tagline: A frontier embodied AI stack built to perceive, simulate, and act in the physical world.

items.title: World simulation
items.description: RTS builds a causal model of how scenes evolve — predicting object motion, interactions, and outcomes before action is taken.

items.title: Live perception
items.description: Reads continuous sensor streams to understand what is in the scene right now, even as lighting, weather, and clutter change.

items.title: Real-time control
items.description: Keeps executing when conditions shift. Perception, prediction, and control stay coupled so systems can operate through uncertainty.

items.title: Physical accuracy
items.description: Trained to advance the state of the art in how faithfully world models capture real physics — not just visual plausibility.

items.title: Unified stack
items.description: Perception, world simulation, and control share one model — so the system that sees the world is the same system that reasons about what happens next.

items.title: Causal scene model
items.description: RTS models what is present, how objects interact, and what follows from action — a foundation for reliable behavior outside the lab.

items.title: Sensor-native runtime
items.description: Built for live operational data: cameras, telemetry, and field signals flowing into a continuous understanding of the environment.

items.title: Sovereign development
items.description: Developed in Norway under national control, with a clear path from research training runs to field deployment.

items.title: Inspection & monitoring
items.description: Understand complex sites over time — detect change, anticipate risk, and support operators with a predictive picture of the environment.

items.title: Logistics & yards
items.description: Model vehicle and equipment movement across busy industrial spaces where planning and safety depend on what happens next.

items.title: Robotics & autonomy
items.description: Give embodied systems a world model they can use to plan, adapt, and keep working when the scene does not match the training demo.

items.title: Field operations
items.description: Bring frontier world-model capability into real environments — outdoor sites, industrial floors, and missions where conditions never stay fixed.

banner.title: Built for the physical world
banner.description: Language models reason about text. Spectr RTS is built to reason about reality — how scenes evolve, how objects interact, and how the world responds to action.

items.question: What is Spectr RTS?
items.answer: Spectr RTS is Spectr's embodied AI stack — a frontier world model that unifies perception, simulation, and real-time control for machines operating in the physical world.

items.question: How is RTS different from a language model?
items.answer: Language models learn from text. RTS learns a causal model of physical scenes: what is present, how things interact, and what happens next — so systems can act, not just describe.

items.question: Where is Spectr RTS being developed?
items.answer: Spectr RTS is developed in Norway. It is early frontier work aimed at field deployment across inspection, logistics, robotics, and outdoor operations.

items.question: Who should get in touch about RTS?
items.answer: Operators, partners, and researchers working on embodied AI, autonomy, or physical-world simulation who want to explore collaboration or early access.

primary.title: Ready to get started?
primary.description: Tell us about your environment and what you want machines to understand and do. We read every message.

secondary.title: Follow the research
secondary.description: Stay close to milestones as we train and harden Spectr RTS for real-world deployment.

SpectrObjectIntelligence.title: Spectr Object Intelligence
SpectrObjectIntelligence.tagline: Real-time object detection for complex physical environments — vehicles, equipment, and activity across industrial and field operations.

items.title: Real-time detection
items.description: Identify vehicles, equipment, people, and activity as they appear in live sensor streams — with latency suited to operational decision-making.

items.title: Multi-object tracking
items.description: Follow targets through occlusions, camera motion, and crowded scenes so operators keep continuity on what matters.

items.title: Industrial robustness
items.description: Built for sites where lighting, weather, dust, and clutter break brittle detectors — keeping a stable read on the scene.

items.title: Structured awareness
items.description: Turn raw pixels into actionable scene state: what is present, where it is, and how activity is unfolding across the site.

items.title: Perception stack
items.description: A production-minded pipeline from sensor input to tracked objects and activity signals ready for dashboards, alerts, and downstream systems.

items.title: Scene understanding
items.description: Goes beyond boxes — models activity and context so detections map to how industrial and field environments actually operate.

items.title: Edge-ready inference
items.description: Designed for deployment close to the cameras and sensors that see the work, not only in a distant data center.

items.title: Built in Norway
items.description: Developed for reliability in the field, with sovereign control over how perception technology is trained and deployed.

items.title: Yards & terminals
items.description: Detect and track vehicles and equipment across large outdoor spaces where throughput and safety depend on knowing what is moving where.

items.title: Industrial sites
items.description: Monitor floors, corridors, and work zones for equipment presence, personnel activity, and operational anomalies.

items.title: Field operations
items.description: Bring reliable object intelligence to remote and outdoor missions where conditions change faster than rule-based systems can keep up.

items.title: Downstream autonomy
items.description: Feed clean, structured perception into planning, alerting, and embodied systems — including Spectr RTS world-model stacks.

banner.title: Perception that holds up in the field
banner.description: Standard detection fails when the environment gets messy. Spectr Object Intelligence is built for the lighting, weather, clutter, and motion of real industrial and field operations.

items.question: What is Spectr Object Intelligence?
items.answer: Spectr Object Intelligence is Spectr's perception stack for live operations — real-time detection and tracking of vehicles, equipment, people, and activity in complex physical environments.

items.question: How is it different from off-the-shelf detectors?
items.answer: It is tuned for industrial and field conditions where lighting, weather, clutter, and motion make generic models brittle, and it outputs structured situational awareness for operators and downstream systems.

items.question: Can it work with Spectr RTS?
items.answer: Yes. Object Intelligence can supply perception to embodied stacks like Spectr RTS, or run as a standalone awareness layer for monitoring and operations.

items.question: Who is it for?
items.answer: Teams running yards, industrial sites, and field operations who need reliable real-time understanding of what is in the scene — and partners exploring perception for autonomy.

primary.title: Ready to get started?
primary.description: Share your site, sensors, and what you need to detect. We will follow up personally.

secondary.title: Explore Spectr RTS
secondary.description: See how our embodied world model builds on perception to simulate and act in the physical world.

items.title: Spectr begins training Spectr RTS
items.summary: We kicked off training of our first general world model, focused on advancing the physical accuracy of world simulation.

Careers.title: Help us build world models
Careers.intro: We're a small, ambitious team in Norway working on one of the hardest and most important problems in AI. If that excites you, we'd like to hear from you.

values.title: Frontier research
values.text: Work on general world models — a genuinely open research frontier with room to make a mark.

values.title: Small team, big scope
values.text: High ownership and real impact. Everyone here shapes what we build and how we build it.

values.title: Built in Norway
values.text: Sovereign technology developed under national control, with a global outlook.

Careers.openText: We hire for talent and drive over titles. If you want to work on world models, get in touch and tell us what you'd want to build.
